comments.author: @TroelsNybo1st
comments.text: Some significant traits of Danish geography are the tunnel valleys which in some cases end up in fjords. Yes, we do have fjords in Denmark. Not as dramatic as the Norwegian ones, but fjords, nevertheless. I was born in a village on top of a watershed between two tunnel valleys, and nowadays I live as a pensioner in another village in a corner of the same ridge. I only need to step out of my front door and walk 200 m to the north to a small bridge across a stream that begins in the western corner of a small lake (Engelsholm sø) and winds to the south, before it turns to the east and follows a broad valley until it runs into Vejle fjord.There are many such places in Denmark.

replies.author: @ane-louisestampe7939
replies.text:
Please come, and DO NOT let the Copenhagen prices scare you off. 
You CAN travel very cheap: You can ride your bike from free shelter to free shelter, and do your shopping in 365 or Netto. There are nice views along ALL the roads and not a town without some FREE sights.

But you can also get some very good train deals, live in B&B's, and eat at ordinary restaurants. Just stay away from Copenhagen and thing that takes man power: It is the salaries that turn up the prices.

Some attractions costs a lot, but you really get value for your money.... You will be there for hours - or the entire day.


Want to zoom in on some animals? Here you go:
Givskud Zoo, Knuthenborg Safaripark, Ree-park, Nordic Dyrepark, Randers Rain Forrest , Odense Zoo and Copenhagen ZOO. All expensive, but you can spend the a day there - and the animals are happy; they are Danish 😉😉 Can't get enough of animals? Go glamping in one of the parks.

If you make it, contact me, and I'll give you a guided VIP-tour in Nyborg 😊
Peace and love

Edit: and DO bring your children. Denmark is one of the most family friendly countries! Children are welcomed everywhere.. except at bars and nightclubs 🙄 But you can take two under 12 with you on all public transport for FREE
